steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the overall structure of the passage. Map the passage in two parts: sentences 1–3 introduce two tribe-operated cultural centers (Hibulb and Turtle Mountain), and the final sentence contrasts both of those centers with non-Indigenous museums.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'designed exhibits of a particular set of artifacts... the same artifacts': the passage never identifies a specific set of artifacts or says the non-Indigenous museums display the same ones. ✗
  • Choice B — 'examines how tribal citizens respond... speculates how non-Indigenous audiences would respond': audience response is never discussed; the passage is about exhibit design strategies, not reactions. ✗
  • Choice C — 'discusses two cultural centers operated by tribes, then compares them with non-Indigenous institutions that present Indigenous exhibits': accurately captures both halves — Hibulb and Turtle Mountain first, then the contrast with non-Indigenous museums. ✓
  • Choice D — 'early strategy... newer strategy': the two tribal centers use a similar strategy, not an old versus new one; the passage never implies a timeline of strategies. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
